label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is about three different jars of low fat milk in a supermarket, and what's the correct statement regarding them. The passage says that all three jars have the same amount of fat. It mentions that the first jar says "1 percent fat," the second says "99 percent fat free," and the third says "LOW FAT" in big letters with "1%" in small letters. Despite the different labels, the text clearly states that all the milk is the same in terms of fat content. But the prices are different, with customers possibly buying the more expensive one because of the labeling. Now, looking at the options: (A) one has more fat than the other two – this contradicts the passage's statement that all have the same fat content. (B) they all cost the same amount of money – the passage says they cost different amounts, so this is wrong. (C) one
